reviews.review_msg:
I learnt so much in Dr. Leroux's class this semester! The class material can be challenging but there are many opportunities to show your understanding (research paper, presentation, and exams). 

I highly recommend taking a class with Dr.Leroux - he is very kind, a great teacher, and supports student learning!

I thought that the course material was challenging, but Dr. Leroux's annotated lectures were great. He uses a lot of examples from actual scientific articles which helps you understand the key concepts highlighted in the lecture. His tests are difficult because they are about thinking critically rather than memorization, but he is a fair marker.

Dr. Leroux is a fantastic professor who is very passionate about what he teaches. His exams are hard but grading is very generous and fair. All the information you need is in the slides. The paper for this course was an awesome learning experience. Would recommend.

Professor Leroux is a really amazing professor. He is so sweet & you can tell by his lectures how fascinated he is with the material in his teachings. He is very approachable & very kind with his grading. He gave us an extension on the term paper too so we can make it better. Overall I would 100% take any class with him again.

Dr. Leroux is an enthusiastic professor with a passion for molecular biology/biochemistry, and teaching. 
He cares deeply about his students, taking time to answer questions clearly and supporting them when challenges arise. 
His flexibility and understanding made a real difference for me, and I truly appreciate his compassion and dedication.

Dr. Leroux is an amazing professor and very passionate about what he teaches. His lectures are very engaging and straight forward, and he always encourages class discussions on the lecture topics, especially after covering a scientific paper. Overall, a great experience, would definitely recommend.

He is so nice and caring. He gave whole class a full week long extension at the end for final paper. Exams were non-cumulative, and he grades them very fairly and always awards tons of partial marks. He is very passionate about the stuff he teaches. Definitely take his class if you could!

Dr. Leroux is a great prof - explains concepts clearly and is very kind and approachable. One of the best profs from MBB that I've had. Quizzes in lab are fair and do-able. Final was tough because of limited time but there were no surprises. Would definitely take another class with Leroux
Demanding but very knowledgeable professor that I highly respect.

I wish this course wasn't so jam-packed so we could have a chance to ask questions. Michel is a fantastic prof who cares deeply about MBB and you can clearly see that in the passion of his teaching. I loved how he took out the final and including four term tests, although they were pretty brutal. The online homework and quizzes were great too!

Prof. Leroux's lectures were dense but he explained concepts clearly. I only ever needed to use his slides for study material as they are very comprehensive. You can tell he cares about the material and also cares about his students. Tests are hard and require you to demonstrate understanding/overlap of multiple concepts.

Dr. Leroux is absolutely amazing. Exams are extremely fair as it's just memorization, expect to know lots of the detail provided in the lectures; absolutely everything ties to each other. He is passionate and caring, ALWAYS responding to your concerns. The class is set up so that you don't need to read the textbook. Class was very smooth sailing.

He is a very reasonable proff..grades u based off multiple assessments so u always have room to boost your grade. his notes are really good and precise..his exams weren't unfair..the content was fairly heavy but he taught it really well.

Michel was able to explain concepts that I had had issues understanding in other courses in ways that I was able to understand immediately. Very impressive lecturer. The class was divided into 4 midterms, online quizzes, and assignments that were just for completion, making it very easy to boost your marks in various ways. 100% recommend.

This is the worst prof I've had since being at sfu. His midterms are super rushed for time and he makes no effort to give them back. He gave an assignment and quiz due over reading week (is that even allowed). After his lectures, I can't even tell you what we've learnt because he tries to cram in so much information so fast and goes overtime.

Dr. Leroux taught the class material through experiment-based examples and encouraged developing important skills through a class presentation and research proposal. His exams were a bit challenging and required a good understanding of the material covered in lecture. The exams focused on application of the material rather than just memorization.
Great course. Critical thinking on how proteins interact and function from biogenesis to degradation, with auxillary components involved (broadly).

Dr. Leroux was a great lecturer. He was always very clear and showed real passion for the material. He includes FYI sections in every set of slides that (usually) apply to the material taught, and were typically interesting. The exams online were rather rough, and his lectures could tend to be long, but overall the course was good and enjoyable.

I took this course as a graduate student. The lectures were very informative and easy to understand. Im normally terrible at test-taking and I did surprisingly well on his exams. Hes very passionate about what he teaches and takes the time to explain concepts clearly. Office hours are especially helpful.

If you put the effort in, you'll do fine. His exams are straightforward; however, doing this course online with him was a disaster and I would personally never take another online course with him. He also does not care enough to accommodate students in case of personal emergencies, even with weeks of notice.

Dr. Leroux is a good professor. The course was quite heavy, but Dr. Leroux explained things pretty clearly. Tests were fair, and weekly assignments/quizzes were easy marks and helped with studying. Slides are very text-heavy and sometimes repetitive, but contain all the info you could need.

I would recommend taking this course with him. It's a content heavy course. He uses slides with a lot of texts and pictures, so even if you don't have time to watch the lectures, the slides would be enough to get the gist of the content. Go to tutorials, memorization alone won't help, you have to fully understand the concepts. Overall a good prof.

Michel is really nice and I love the extra notes that he drafts up which are super helpful and makes the material easy to understand. He can be a bit disorganized at times but he does listen to student feedback. I would definitely consider taking another class with him!

A very reasonable prof. You decide what your grade will be depending on your habits. Keep up, and you'll do great. No trick questions. There is a lot of material but that's with any MBB course. Exams are very fair. Open book quiz and completion marked assignment every week (aka free marks). He makes lectures interesting and explains well.

explains concepts super well. lots of information to learn but it's super interesting anyway. exams are super straightforward and easy if you read his slides. studying a day or 2 before class can get you an A- on the exam. weekly quizzes and assignments help with studying and it also gives you free marks (basically an extra 20%)

One of the most caring profs ever. really shows a love for science. Wants to see his students succeed which is honestly refreshing at this school. Quizzes are quite tough but he makes sure grading in the end is fair.

Dr. Leroux is the most sincere and caring prof Ive had during my time at SFU. He explains all concepts exceptionally well and encourages questions during and after class. If you ever have a chance to take his class, do not hesitate. The grading was clear and there are many opportunities for you to get a decent grade.
Dr. Leroux is very enthusiastic about the subject, and explains concepts very clearly. Lectures are very useful, and he gives good feedback. Don't skip lectures and do put in an effort.

Of the 5 MBB courses I've taken, this was by far my most successful. The grading is split across a very diverse breakdown, giving plenty of opportunity to earn a decent grade. Dr Leroux's lectures & feedback give you literally everything that you need to succeed in this course. Don't skip lectures, stay on top of the work, & you will be rewarded.

Dr. Leroux was clearly very enthusiastic about this course. He was patient and always willing to help troubleshoot problems during the labs. Some of the quizzes were a little tricky but the lowest one ended up be dropped anyway. Make sure to attend lecture as they are not recorded and he might drop some hints about the quizzes and final.

Dr. Leroux was an excellent prof for MBB309W. The course itself is a lot of work, but the techniques are very useful and Dr. Leroux was very approachable and knowledgable whenever I asked for help. Weekly quizzes, final quiz, and lab reports were all graded fairly and ample time was given to complete each one. Would recommend!

I enjoyed having Dr. Leroux for MBB 309W. The labs were long (most go beyond 4 hours) and it was a writing-intensive course. However Dr. Leroux was VERY patient during labs and was always available for help and feedback. The weekly quizzes were not easy, but they prepared us well for the final quiz which was very fair.

Dr. Leroux made the labs and classes enjoyable and he was always available for help and feedback on assignments. This was very helpful as the class is a 4 credit W science course and a lot of material is covered. His clear and concise breakdowns of the assignments helped a lot as well. I would highly recommend taking MBB309W with Dr. Leroux.

Dr. Leroux was an amazing prof to take this course with. He was passionate about the material and about sharing some of his own research as well. Labs are long, but he never rushed anyone and helped out as much as possible. Lab reports are time consuming and lengthy, but that is the nature of the field. Overall great prof, and would take again.

Dr Leroux is very enthusiastic and passionate about the material he teaches and genuinely wants to see students succeed. He is approachable and friendly as well. The course is hard work, the labs are long but Dr Leroux and the TA allowed us to stay overtime and stayed until everyone was done. The marking of assignments and exams was very fair.

Very clear in lecture to prepare students for the lab portion of the class. Really wants students to understand biochemistry to prepare them for future endeavours, instead of regurgitating material. Final was difficult because it reinforced thinking about the "why" instead of straight memorization, but fair. Also, has a great sense of humour.

I found 309W a really enjoyable course and that was mainly due to Dr Leroux. Since 309W was an upper level MBB course a lot of material was covered but Dr Leroux's enthusiasm made the class enjoyable. He was really open to questions both during and after class. The course was very information dense so this made a huge difference as well.

Although the material can be a bit tricky at times, Dr. Leroux always takes the time in lecture to go over all aspects of the next lab and explains everything very clearly to make sure no one is lost or flustered during lab times. He is clearly very knowledgeable and very enthusiastic about the material he teaches and is always available to help.

Dr. Leroux is a great professor. He ensures that the students leaving his class have a firm understanding of the theoretical and practical aspects of the course concepts and are able to operate well in a lab environment. He was also always present and enthusiastic to answer any questions in lab. I highly recommend taking courses with Dr. Leroux.
Wasn't a fan of his lectures, but his tests were mostly straightforward.

A fine prof but if you can find a better one then I definitely recommend avoiding Leroux. He reads from the slides (which aren't his own) and goes really slowly in a super soft and monotone voice, which put half his class to sleep. Only reason why he was an okay prof was that his exams were fair and it wasn't too difficult to get a decent mark.

Dry content, each lecture was on average 40 slides packed full of text, which makes it a very material dense course. Most work I've ever put into a course in my life with discouraging results. Do not recommend. Not curved. Spring 2017 Final average was 58%.
Dr. Leroux is a nice guy but lectures get very dry.  LOTS of material.

Michel is a teacher who is genuinely excited about the subject. A little shy and self-deprecating, he tries hard to make the material make sense, and is always willing to go over things between class. That said, it IS an information-dense subject, so a good bit of reading and work on your own is required to do well. I found study groups helpful.

Dr. Leroux is a great prof. He made a course like MBB which is filled with details very interesting and fascinating. Truly a legend when it comes down to presenting lectures in the most effective and simplistic manner. If you go over his lecture notes thoroughly, you should be able to earn a decent grade. Had great time with him this spring!

There is a lot of material covered in MBB222, but Dr. Leroux makes an incredible effort to try and present the info as clear as possible. PP tend to be long and wordy, but if you follow thru w all the slides, you will not need to dwell in the textbook.

Dr. Leroux is a great professor. He went through the course material clearly and in an organized manner. He was also present during the labs, and is willing to answer questions both in lecture and in lab. I think a huge majority of students do well in his class but don't end up writing reviews, making this site unrepresentative of his teaching.

Dr Leroux is a wonderfully hilarious professor who not only discusses lecture content in crystal clear detail, but also takes time to get to know his students in the lab! He made MBB309 very enjoyable, and I am glad to have taken this course with him as opposed to other (more difficult) MBB profs! I would definitely recommend taking 309W with him!

Dr. Leroux is a fantastic professor, who is not only incredibly knowledgeable, but genuinely cares that you succeed in the course. MBB 309 is a lab course, so you need to come prepared, having already read and understood the purpose of each step in the experimental protocol. I learned so much in this course and a lot of that is credit to Dr. Leroux

Honestly, I can't speak for people taking Upper Division classes with Dr. Leroux, but as someone in his MBB222 class, I think he's one of the best you can get. He can be a bit dry at times, and his lectures can be quite heavy, but that's MBB. It's a dense course and Dr. Leroux is SUPER approachable and friendly. Easiest and most friendly prof ever.
I wish Dr. Leroux would teach more upper MBB courses. Prepare well for the final quiz, which can be challenging.

Basically taught myself the whole course out of the textbook. His exams are absolutely brutal and he asks the most irrelevant questions about topics discussed for 5 minutes on 1 slide. He's so vague and not great at explaining, even when prompted.
hardest final ive ever done in my entire life. two girls cried. I recommend you not take it with him.
hard tests, not enough materials to help you

I retook this course so i had Dr. Beh's notes for the same slides he used. The courses is mostly self study so to get close to avg you need to really understand the material. Finals were complicated indirect questions from multiple mini topics. sample tutorial questions mostly irrelevant except for PCR, gel reading, and restriction mapping.

I retook this course so i know he used Dr. Beh's notes who gave me notes for the same slides he used. Mostly self study.to get close to avg you need to really understand the material. Final were complicated indirect questions from multiple mini topics.sample tutorial questions mostly irrelevant except for PCR, gel reading, and restriction mapping.
brutal exams,specially final! still shocked!
He was overall an okay prof, he seemed to enjoy the subject matter (which was tricky!)...exams were tough, especially the final!
Very interesting course material but hard exams. Take this class if you are hardcore biochem major. Otherwise I wouldn't recommend it. Seems like he picks favorites from the class
exams are hard - dont bother memorizing every little detail; lectures were interesting in the first half of the semester - second half got a little crazy; overall very interesting class
No Comments
